question_text: A solar panel has a maximum power output of 300 W. Over a year, it receives an average of 4 hours of useful sunlight per day. Calculate how many watt-hours of energy the panel produces in one day.
correct_answer: Energy = Power × Time = 300 W × 4 hours = 1200 Wh (or 1.2 kWh)
explanation: Energy is calculated by multiplying power (in watts) by time (in hours). The solar panel operates at 300 W for 4 hours, giving 1200 watt-hours of energy per day.
